contributions.description: Foundational Kabbalistic text (~1st-3rd century CE). Source for 3 mother letters (elements), 7 double letters (planets), 12 simple letters (zodiac), and 231 Gates (two-letter combinations).

contributions.description: Published the Tree of Life path layout (Kircher Tree) that became the standard Western Kabbalistic topology: 22 paths connecting 10 Sefirot.

contributions.work: The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential
contributions.description: Systematic harmonic analysis of tides. Classified tidal constituents (M2, S2, N2, K1, O1, etc.) with periods and Doodson numbers. Foundation of modern tidal prediction.

references.note: 5 tidal constituents: M2, S2, N2, K1, O1 periods and harmonic analysis

contributions.description: Public domain tidal constituent amplitudes and periods. CO-OPS tide prediction methodology.

contributions.description: Revised TSI measurement from SORCE/TIM: 1361 W/m^2 at 1 AU

contributions.description: Atmospheric transmittance model for surface irradiance: I_surface = I_toa * 0.7^(AM^0.678). Empirical fit for clear-sky solar radiation through Earth's atmosphere.

contributions.work: Applied Solar Energy: An Introduction (co-author with Aden Meinel)
contributions.description: Atmospheric transmittance model for surface irradiance
Standard atmospheric lapse rate of -6.5 C per 1000m (troposphere). Used worldwide for altitude-temperature relationship.

references.note: Lapse rate -6.5 C/km for elevation temperature correction

contributions.work: The I Ching and the Genetic Code
contributions.description: First systematic mapping of 64 DNA codons to 64 I Ching hexagrams via binary correspondence. Both systems enumerate 2^6=64 states: codon=3 bases x 2 bits, hexagram=6 lines x 1 bit.

references.note: Schonberger binary mapping: RNA bases to yin/yang lines (U=00, C=01, G=10, A=11). Note: A and G swapped vs our enum order.

contributions.description: Used shadow angles at Alexandria and Syene to compute Earth's circumference: 39,375 km (actual 40,075 km) — 1.7% error with a gnomon and geometry.
Compiled first systematic star catalog (~850 stars). Discovered precession of equinoxes (~46 arcsec/year). Measured tropical year to 6.5-minute accuracy.

references.note: Tropical year measurement and precession discovery

contributions.description: Earth circumference 39,968 km (0.27% error). Sidereal year 365.25858 days (3.2-min error). Equation of center 1200 years before Kepler. Sine tables. Earth rotates on axis.

references.note: Earth circumference, sidereal year, lunar month measurements
Digest 041: Complete mathematical chapter via Bhaskara I commentary — sine table, pi, pulverizer, constants

contributions.description: Oldest surviving Sanskrit prose commentary on mathematics. 24-entry sine table construction, pi debate (3.1416 vs √10), pulverizer (kuttaka) algorithm with 22 astronomical examples, eclipse geometry, arithmetic series formulas, Rule of Three hierarchy. Aryabhata's constants: civil days 1,577,917,500 per yuga.
Academic translation of Bhaskara I's commentary on the Aryabhatiya mathematical chapter. Two volumes: translation + supplements.

references.note: Digest 041: Translator of Bhaskara I commentary

contributions.description: Lunar month 29.530582 days (0.6-second error). Rules for zero and negative numbers. Interpolation formula.
Established the fixed Hebrew calendar (359 CE). Molad (lunar month) 29.530594 days — 0.4-second monthly error. Still used today.
Astronomical tables used for 400+ years. Sine tables to 4 decimal places. Algorithms so foundational the word 'algorithm' derives from his name.

contributions.work: On the Sizes and Distances of the Sun and Moon
contributions.description: First geometric method for measuring Earth-Sun distance. Estimated 19x lunar distance (actual ~389x). Method was correct; measurement limited by naked-eye precision.
Sidereal year 365.25636 days — essentially exact match to modern value. Star catalog of 1018 stars. Built 40-meter sextant at Samarkand.

references.note: Sidereal year measurement — essentially exact (1437 CE)

contributions.description: Tropical year 365.2436 days (2-min error). Built world's first astronomical clock tower with escapement mechanism.

references.note: Tropical year measurement via clock tower (1088 CE)

contributions.description: Heliocentric model. Sidereal year 365.25671 days. Reframed the entire solar system.
Star positions accurate to 1 arcminute — most accurate naked-eye observations ever recorded. Enabled Kepler's laws.
Advised Julius Caesar on calendar reform (46 BCE). Designed 365.25-day solar year with leap year every 4 years.
Proposed the World Calendar (1930): 4 identical quarters of 91 days + Worldsday. Nearly adopted by League of Nations 1937.
Proposed 13 months of 28 days + Year Day (1902). 13th month 'Sol' between June and July. Used by Kodak internally 1928-1989.
Four Noble Truths, Eightfold Path. Buddhist calendar and Uposatha observance days derive from his teachings.

references.note: Buddhist system origin node in knowledge graph

contributions.description: Primary text of Kabbalah. Systematized the Sefirot, paths, and Four Worlds framework.

references.note: Kabbalah origin node — influenced Isaac Luria

contributions.description: Developed the concepts of tzimtzum (contraction), shevirat ha-kelim (breaking of vessels), and tikkun (repair). Transformed Kabbalistic thought.

references.note: Kabbalah transmission node — influenced by Moses de Leon

contributions.description: Promulgated the Gregorian calendar (1582). Deleted 10 days, adjusted leap year rules. Calendar still used worldwide.

references.note: Gregorian system node in knowledge graph

contributions.description: Originated the method of transposing planetary orbital periods to audible frequencies via octave doubling (f × 2^n). Derived Earth year = 136.1 Hz (C#, 'Om' frequency), Earth day = 194.18 Hz (G), synodic month = 210.42 Hz (G#).

references.note: Planetary frequency mapping via octave transposition (Cosmic Octave method)

contributions.work: Secular terms of classical planetary theories
contributions.description: High-precision 10th-degree polynomial for mean obliquity of the ecliptic, valid over 10000 years from J2000.0. Published in Astronomy and Astrophysics Vol. 157, p. 68.
Computus algorithm for Easter date calculation (Gregorian calendar), devised in 1876, published pp. 73-74. Valid from 1583 onward with no exceptions, unlike Gauss's method.
Comprehensive study of Egyptian decan star groups, diagonal star clocks, and astronomical ceiling texts. Established the standard transliteration system for the 36 decan names.
Co-author with Neugebauer. Identified decan star groups in coffin lids, temple ceilings, and papyri. Established chronological framework for Egyptian astronomical observation.

contributions.work: It's About Time: Ancient Egyptian Cosmology
contributions.description: Modern star identifications for Egyptian decans, correlating ancient star group descriptions with contemporary astronomical catalogs.

contributions.work: In Search of Cosmic Order: Selected Essays on Egyptian Archaeoastronomy
contributions.description: Decan-star correlation survey establishing consensus identifications for Egyptian decan groups with modern stars and constellations.
Definitive scholarship on the Zoroastrian Yazdgerdi calendar: epoch, month/day names, Gatha days, seasonal festivals, and calendar reform history.

references.note: Yazdgerdi calendar structure, epoch, month/day naming conventions

contributions.description: Data visualization principles: escaping flatland (multidimensional data on 2D), micro/macro readings, layering and separation, small multiples, 1+1=3 interference effect, Imhof's 4 color rules, 20-30 color limit, data-ink ratio, nature's palette strategy.

references.note: Complete extraction: 6 chapters, layering/separation/color/small multiples principles

contributions.description: 4 color rules for information display: bright colors sparingly on muted backgrounds, avoid adjacent light bright colors, large areas muted / small areas bright, interweave colors carpet-fashion for unity.

references.note: Imhof's 4 rules quoted via Tufte pp. 82-83

contributions.description: Systematic color interaction study: simultaneous contrast mechanism, subtraction rule, Weber-Fechner perceptual law application to color, color relativity (8 illusion types), Schopenhauer area ratios, three color polarities (light-dark, warm-cool, wet-dry), rejection of mechanical harmony rules in favor of quantity-based color relationships.

references.note: Full 26-chapter extraction: Q1-Q5 on contrast, relativity, temperature, quantitative rules, harmony

contributions.work: The Laws of Contrast of Colour
contributions.description: Pioneering simultaneous contrast research. Graduated grey scale method. Foundation for Impressionist color theory and all subsequent color interaction study.

references.note: Referenced by Albers Ch XX for graduated scale method

contributions.description: Color quantity ratios for visual balance from Goethe's 6-part color circle: Y:O:R:V:B:G = 3:4:6:9:8:6. Complementary balance areas: lightest color gets smallest area, darkest gets largest.

references.note: Schopenhauer area ratios quoted by Albers Ch XVI

contributions.description: Discovery that changing or adding a single color can transform an entire color composition. A special case of optical mixture, distinct from simultaneous contrast.

references.note: Bezold Effect documented by Albers Ch XIII

contributors.name: Ernst Heinrich Weber & Gustav Theodor Fechner

contributions.description: Perception follows logarithmic function of stimulus: geometric physical progression produces arithmetically equal perceptual steps. Applies to light, color, sound, weight, temperature. Foundation for perceptually uniform scales.

references.note: Weber-Fechner Law applied to color by Albers Ch XX

contributions.work: A Short History of Nearly Everything
contributions.description: Deep time scale comparisons, geochronology timeline, Big Bang nucleosynthesis narrative, extinction data, cosmic distance analogies. Source of vivid metaphors for earth_drama and fun_facts modules.

references.note: Scale comparisons, geochronology, fun facts extraction

contributions.work: Age of meteorites and the Earth
contributions.description: Definitive measurement of Earth's age at 4.55 billion years using lead isotope ratios from Canyon Diablo meteorite. Also discovered atmospheric lead contamination from leaded gasoline.
Calculated background extinction rate of 1 species per 4 years on average throughout biological history. Framework for measuring human impact on biodiversity (120,000x background rate).

references.note: Extinction rate data for fun_facts module

contributions.work: Various Eras and Calendars Used in the Countries of Islam
contributions.description: Definitive study of Yazdgerdi calendar epoch (JD 1952063.5 = 16 June 632 CE), 365-day no-leap structure, Zoroastrian calendar mechanics.
Lead author of the IAU 2006 general precession polynomial adopted as the international standard for precession of the equinoxes. Capitaine, Wallace & Chapront (2003).
Co-author of IAU 2006 precession model. Developed high-precision lunar theory (ELP/MPP02) and contributed to fundamental astronomical constants.

Comprehensive commentary on the Kalachakra Tantra covering Buddhist cosmology, astronomical time cycles, four mandalas, Mount Meru structure, 25 Kalki chronology, word-numeral encoding, and six elements correspondence. The most detailed Tibetan exposition of Kalachakra external cosmology.

contributions.work: Translation of Ornament of Stainless Light
contributions.description: English translation of Khedrup Norsang Gyatso's commentary on the Kalachakra Tantra. Library of Tibetan Classics, Volume 14. Includes appendixes on time measurement, linear measurement, word-numerals, and Kalki chronology.

references.note: Translator of primary source for digest 042

contributions.description: Designer of the French Republican calendar and proposer of the Romme leap year rule (Gregorian rule applied to year+1)
GLSL simplex noise implementation based on Perlin's simplex noise, widely used in shader programming

references.note: Simplex noise GLSL implementation (Ashima Arts / MIT license)

contributions.description: Co-author of the widely-used GLSL noise library with Stefan Gustavson (Ashima Arts)

references.note: Co-author of simplex noise GLSL implementation (Ashima Arts / MIT license)

contributions.work: GPU Gems 2 Ch.16: Accurate Atmospheric Scattering
contributions.description: Real-time atmospheric scattering shaders: Rayleigh + Mie with exponential density falloff, lookup table optimization, inner/outer atmosphere views

references.note: GPU Gems 2 Ch.16 algorithm extraction for planet limb glow and sky rendering

contributions.work: A Practical Analytic Model for Daylight
contributions.description: Analytical sky color model: Perez luminance distribution, turbidity-based Rayleigh/Mie coefficients, zenith color computation (SIGGRAPH 99)

references.note: Preetham sky model for analytical daylight rendering

contributions.work: Display of the Earth Taking into Account Atmospheric Scattering
contributions.description: Foundational paper on rendering planetary atmospheres with physically-based Rayleigh and Mie scattering, optical depth integration along view rays

contributions.work: Theory of light scattering by particles small compared to wavelength
contributions.description: Rayleigh scattering: intensity proportional to 1/lambda^4, explains why the sky is blue. Fundamental to all atmospheric rendering.

references.note: Rayleigh scattering physics used in atmosphere shaders

contributions.work: Mie theory of electromagnetic scattering by spherical particles
contributions.description: Mie scattering: wavelength-independent scattering by particles comparable to wavelength size (dust, aerosols). Forward-biased. Explains haze and sun glare.

references.note: Mie scattering physics used in atmosphere shaders

contributors.name: Louis G. Henyey & Jesse L. Greenstein

contributions.description: Henyey-Greenstein phase function: single-parameter model for anisotropic scattering, widely used in atmospheric and volumetric rendering

references.note: HG phase function for Mie scattering direction

contributions.description: Clean MIT-licensed GLSL implementation of ray-marched atmospheric scattering with configurable planet/atmosphere parameters

contributions.work: Fast blur techniques for real-time rendering
contributions.description: Kawase and Dual Kawase blur: progressive downsample/upsample blur approximation, faster than Gaussian with acceptable quality for bloom effects (GDC 2003)

references.note: Dual Kawase blur for bloom pipeline

contributions.work: ACES Filmic Tone Mapping Curve fit
contributions.description: Practical fitted approximation of ACES RRT+ODT: 5 coefficients, max error 0.0138, public domain (CC0/MIT). Industry standard for real-time HDR rendering.

references.note: ACES filmic tone mapping curve for HDR rendering

contributions.description: Parametric filmic curve with shoulder/toe/linear sections for cinematic HDR rendering. Used in Uncharted 2/3, widely adopted in game industry.
Single-pass screen-space edge detection and blurring along edge direction. 9 texture lookups per pixel. Industry standard post-process AA.

contributions.work: Advanced HDR Color Pipelines tone mapping
contributions.description: Parametric tone mapping operator with configurable mid-tone anchor, HDR max, and shoulder (AMD, GDC 2016)

references.note: FXAA anti-aliasing and Lottes tone mapping operator

contributions.work: HDR Theory and Practice (Gran Turismo tone mapping)
contributions.description: Three-segment tone mapping curve (toe/linear/shoulder) with 6 controllable parameters. Designed for automotive visualization. Polyphony Digital / Gran Turismo.
Modern tone mapper with superior color preservation. Log2 encoding + polynomial sigmoid + color space transforms. Avoids ACES hue shifts on saturated colors. Adopted by Blender and Three.js.

references.note: AgX tone mapping for accurate HDR color rendering

contributions.description: Comprehensive OpenGL/GLSL tutorial series including bloom, HDR, PBR, shadows. Standard reference for learning real-time graphics.

references.note: Bloom pipeline and HDR rendering tutorials

contributions.work: Domain warping and procedural noise techniques
contributions.description: Pioneered domain warping ('warp by fbm of fbm') for organic cloud and terrain shapes. Co-created Shadertoy. Extensive library of signed distance functions, noise algorithms, and procedural techniques.

references.note: Domain warping technique for gas giant cloud patterns
Domain warping, cosine palettes, FBM for Milky Way rendering
Voronoise, domain warping, smooth minimum noise functions
Domain warping for solar turbulence, Voronoi convection cells

contributions.work: Procedural gas giant rendering with GPU noise
contributions.description: Latitude-stretched FBM noise technique for gas giant banding: asymmetric frequency scaling creates horizontal bands, domain warping adds turbulence

Invented gradient noise (1983, Academy Award 1997). Foundation of all procedural texturing in computer graphics. Simplex noise (2001) improvement with better isotropy and lower computational complexity.

references.note: Gradient noise inventor, referenced by all GPU noise implementations
Foundation for all procedural sun turbulence

contributions.description: Voronoi-based cellular noise function (SIGGRAPH 1996). Produces organic cell patterns, cracked surfaces, and biological textures. F1/F2 distance metrics enable diverse visual effects.

references.note: Cellular/Worley noise inventor — convection cells, terrain

contributions.description: Platform-stable GPU hash functions that avoid sin(), which produces different results across GPU vendors. Polynomial dot-product hashing for cross-platform deterministic noise.

references.note: Hash Without Sine — cross-platform GPU random number generation

contributions.description: Optimized GPU noise library with Value, Perlin, Cellular, and Simplex noise in 2D/3D/4D. Multiple hash backends for quality/performance tradeoffs.

contributions.work: Red Blob Games — noise and terrain tutorials
contributions.description: Interactive visual tutorials on noise combination strategies, ridge noise, power curves, octave independence. Landmark pedagogy for procedural generation.

references.note: Noise combination pedagogy for terrain/galaxy textures

contributions.description: Temperature-to-color mapping for stars, corona fragment shader with noise-driven rays. Three.js implementation of procedural star with blackbody color.

references.note: Temperature-based corona color, star rendering architecture

contributions.description: 3D FBM surface technique for sun rendering with Fresnel glow. Step-by-step shader tutorial for realistic solar surface.

references.note: 3D FBM sun surface technique, Fresnel edge glow

contributions.work: Curl noise for procedural fluid motion
contributions.description: Divergence-free noise field via curl operator. Produces fluid-like, incompressible flow patterns. Foundation for procedural fluid simulation in graphics.

references.note: Curl noise algorithm for fluid-like procedural motion
Curl noise for solar flow fields

contributions.description: Influential procedural sun/star shader with FBM turbulence, corona rays, and temperature-based coloring. Widely forked reference implementation.

references.note: Main Sequence Star — reference sun shader implementation

contributions.description: Billboard rendering of perfect spheres using ray-sphere intersection in the fragment shader. Single quad renders a pixel-perfect sphere.

references.note: Sphere impostor rendering for efficient sun billboard

contributions.description: Authoritative Go implementation of Jean Meeus's Astronomical Algorithms, including Jewish and Moslem calendar routines (Chapter 9).

references.note: Reference source for Meeus Ch9 algorithm verification

contributions.description: Comprehensive web-based calendar converter implementing multiple calendar algorithms. Reference implementation for calendar cross-validation.

references.note: Calendar converter reference for Jewish/Islamic algorithm validation

contributions.work: Islamic astronomical tables and intercalary schemes
contributions.description: 9th-century Islamic astronomer. Refined Ptolemaic parameters. Contributed to intercalary cycle schemes used in the Islamic tabular calendar.

references.note: Intercalary Scheme II for Islamic calendar

contributions.work: Basic Color Terms: Their Universality and Evolution
contributions.description: Landmark study identifying 11 universal basic color categories across 98 languages. Established evolutionary ordering of color term acquisition.

references.note: 11 universal basic color terms — cross-cultural color categories

contributions.work: Basic Color Terms: Their Universality and Evolution (co-author with Berlin)
contributions.description: Co-established the universal color categories theory. Later expanded to 16 categories with the World Color Survey.
Proposed red-green, blue-yellow, black-white opponent channels in color perception. Physiological basis for Berlin & Kay color universals.

references.note: Opponent-process theory — physiological basis of color perception

contributions.description: Definitive reference on gamut mapping algorithms: clipping, compression, directional strategies for handling out-of-gamut colors.

contributions.work: The Chakras and the Human Energy Fields (co-author)
contributions.description: Neuropsychiatric perspective on chakra system. Clinical observations of energy field structure, endocrine gland correspondences, five energy body layers.

references.note: 7 chakra data table, endocrine mappings, 5 energy body layers

contributions.work: The Chakras and the Human Energy Fields (co-author)
contributions.description: Detailed observational descriptions of chakra colors, rotation patterns, and energy field layers. Co-founded Therapeutic Touch modality.

references.note: Chakra observation data, energy body layer properties

contributions.description: Developed lunar theory and the lunation numbering system (Brown Lunation Number) starting from the first new moon of 1923. Standard reference for counting synodic months.

references.note: Brown Lunation Number epoch and calculation

contributions.work: The Chinese Sky During the Han (with Jacob Kistemaker)
contributions.description: Definitive scholarly reconstruction of Chinese 28 Xiu (lunar lodges) with historical determinative star positions and unequal lodge widths.

references.note: Chinese Xiu (28 lodges) ecliptic positions and spans

contributions.work: Kitab suwar al-kawakib (Book of Fixed Stars)
contributions.description: Comprehensive star catalog that formalized Arabic lunar mansions (Manzil). First recorded observation of the Andromeda Galaxy and the Large Magellanic Cloud.

references.note: Arabic Manzil (28 lunar mansions) names and tradition

contributions.description: Overview first, zoom and filter, then details-on-demand. Foundational principle for interactive information visualization and Time's progressive disclosure UI.

references.note: Overview first, zoom and filter — governs UI progressive disclosure

contributions.work: About Face: The Essentials of Interaction Design
contributions.description: Sovereign posture application design, modeless feedback, elimination of navigational excise. Informs Time's full-screen artwork-first interaction pattern.

references.note: Sovereign posture — Time is a full-screen artwork, not a dashboard

contributions.description: Visual hierarchy (3-color text, weight>size), spacing system (16px base, 25% minimum gap), color palette (9-shade HSL), depth/shadow patterns
Button depth (two-part shadows), elevation system (5 levels), perceived brightness, dark theme patterns
Trunk test (6 navigation questions), self-evidence continuum, three facts of web use (scan/satisfice/muddle), reservoir of goodwill (diminishers/increasers), billboard design principle

contributions.work: Color Imaging: Fundamentals and Applications (co-author with Reinhard)
contributions.description: CIE color space formulas, color difference metrics, gamut mapping algorithms

contributions.work: Color Imaging: Fundamentals and Applications (co-author with Reinhard)
contributions.description: CIE color space formulas, color difference metrics, gamut mapping algorithms

contributions.work: Color Imaging: Fundamentals and Applications (co-author with Reinhard)
contributions.description: CIE color space formulas, color difference metrics, gamut mapping algorithms

contributions.work: The Coming of the Golden Age
contributions.description: Independently proposed I Ching / DNA parallel in 1969. UC Berkeley molecular biologist who recognized binary isomorphism between 64 codons and 64 hexagrams.

references.note: Independent parallel discovery cited in Schonberger epilogue

contributions.work: Epilogue to The I Ching and the Genetic Code
contributions.description: Sinological analysis resolving Stent vs Schonberger apparent discrepancy as printing error.

references.note: Epilogue reconciling Stent and Schonberger mappings

contributions.work: Introduction to The I Ching and the Genetic Code
contributions.description: Connected I Ching, DNA, and Tantric Buddhist philosophy.

references.note: Introduction connecting I Ching / DNA / Buddhist philosophy

contributions.work: Two Letters on the Binary Number System and Chinese Philosophy
contributions.description: Identified binary number system / I Ching hexagram parallel in 1713. Recognized Fu Hsi sequence as binary counting 0-63.

references.note: Binary number system / I Ching parallel (1713)

contributions.description: Discovered biophoton emissions from DNA. Wave-system research confirmed vibrational/informational aspect of genetic code.

references.note: Biophoton research confirming DNA informational properties

contributions.work: Mapping Time: The Calendar and its History
contributions.description: Comprehensive calendar algorithms, Julian Day to Gregorian conversion algorithm used in celtic_tree and other calendar modules

references.note: Richards algorithm for JD to Gregorian conversion

contributions.work: Horizontal Diagram of the Sixty-Four Gua
contributions.description: Discovered binary arrangement of 64 hexagrams ~600 years before Leibniz. Greatest scholar of the Symbol and Number School. Connected He Tu/Lo Shu patterns to trigram arrangements.

references.note: Binary representation of hexagrams validates Shao Yong's sequence

contributions.work: The Quintessence of He Tu and Lo Shu
contributions.description: Qing dynasty scholar (1681-1762) who summarized Shao Yong's discoveries about the connection between He Tu, Lo Shu, and the Eight Primary Gua arrangements

references.note: Referenced in Huang's Numerology of the I Ching

contributions.work: Transmission of I Ching diagrams to Leibniz
contributions.description: Jesuit missionary serving in the court of Emperor Kangxi (1662-1713). Recognized parallel between Leibniz's binary system and Shao Yong's hexagram sequence, sent diagrams to Leibniz.

references.note: Bridge between Eastern and Western binary mathematics

contributions.description: Comprehensive academic study of Burmese, Thai, Lao, and Cambodian calendar systems. Documented intercalation rules, lunisolar structure, and year-type calculations for all SE Asian Buddhist calendars.

references.note: SE Asian Buddhist calendar structure and BE offset

contributors.name: Donald A. Norman
contributors.contribution: The Design of Everyday Things (1988) — affordances, natural mapping, visibility, feedback, Gulf of Execution/Evaluation, seven principles of user-centered design

contributors.name: Lloydine Argüelles
contributors.role: Co-creator of Dreamspell and Telektonon systems
contributors.contribution: Telektonon: El Juego de la Profecía (1995) — co-designed the 28-day prophecy board game, 52-card system, synchronometric daily practice. Co-created Dreamspell with José Argüelles.
contributors.source: Telektonon El Juego De La Profecia

contributors.contribution: Spanish translation of the Telektonon manual (1995)
contributors.source: Telektonon El Juego De La Profecia

contributors.contribution: Design support for Telektonon board game (1995)
contributors.source: Telektonon El Juego De La Profecia

contributors.name: Raphil Lee
contributors.contribution: Design support for Telektonon board game (1995)
contributors.source: Telektonon El Juego De La Profecia

contributors.name: International Union of Crystallography (IUCr)
contributors.contribution: Crystal system classification (6 systems: cubic, hexagonal, tetragonal, orthorhombic, monoclinic, triclinic) used in Diamond Room procedural geometry

contributors.name: Bill Smith
contributors.contribution: 40+ year daily I Ching practitioner who helped Huang find authentic English names for the 64 gua

contributors.name: Master Yin
contributors.contribution: Revered Taoist teacher who transmitted esoteric I Ching knowledge in secret during 1960s China, taught Alfred Huang the authentic tradition

contributors.contribution: UI design pattern catalog: Safe Exploration, Instant Gratification, Satisficing, Deferred Choices, Responsive Disclosure, Good Defaults, Dashboard, Settings Editor, Wizard — foundational patterns for Time's onboarding, settings, and personal space UX
